modules.title: CX Standards, Complaints & Etiquette
modules.description: Complaint levels and empowerment, absolute NOs, pro tips, and etiquette guidelines for social media, telephone, and café interactions.

chunks.title: Complaint Levels & Empowerment
chunks.intent: Define the three levels of complaints and who is empowered to handle them.
chunks.content: Complaints are classified into three levels. Level 1 — Taste & Personal Preference: Examples include dessert too sweet or bitter, coffee too strong or weak, cheesecake could be colder, brownie texture feels too dense, presentation could look better. Empowered roles: Basic Barista, Advanced Barista, Buddy Trainer, Shift Manager, Assistant Store Manager, Store Manager. Expected response: Listen politely, acknowledge feedback, and adjust if possible (less sugar, remake with preference, offer alternatives). Level 2 — Product or Service Issue: Examples include wrong dessert or drink served, item missing from order, stale pastry or melted ice cream, long wait time without communication, poor dessert plating or messy presentation. Empowered roles: Advanced Barista, Buddy Trainer, Shift Manager, Assistant Store Manager, Store Manager. Expected response: Apologize, correct the mistake immediately, remake or replace the item, inform the shift lead if needed. Partial refund can be processed by Shift Manager and above. Level 3 — Safety & Compliance Concern: Examples include allergen contamination (nuts, dairy, gluten), foreign object in dessert or drink, spoiled or expired food served, customer reports illness after consuming food, harassment or unsafe behaviour in the café. Empowered roles: Assistant Store Manager, Store Manager, Operations Manager. Expected response: Escalate immediately to Store Manager or Operations Manager. Follow incident reporting procedures.

qa_pairs.instruction: What are the levels of customer complaints at TWC?
qa_pairs.output: There are three levels. Level 1: Taste & Personal Preference (e.g., coffee too strong, dessert too sweet). Level 2: Product or Service Issue (e.g., wrong item served, long wait). Level 3: Safety & Compliance Concern (e.g., allergen contamination, foreign objects, illness).

qa_pairs.instruction: Who can handle a Level 1 complaint?
qa_pairs.output: Level 1 (Taste & Personal Preference) can be handled by any role: Basic Barista, Advanced Barista, Buddy Trainer, Shift Manager, Assistant Store Manager, or Store Manager. Listen politely, acknowledge feedback, and adjust if possible.

qa_pairs.instruction: Who can process a partial refund for a customer?
qa_pairs.output: Partial refunds can only be processed by Shift Manager and above. This applies to Level 2 complaints (product or service issues).

qa_pairs.instruction: What constitutes a Level 3 complaint?
qa_pairs.output: Level 3 — Safety & Compliance Concerns include: allergen contamination (nuts, dairy, gluten), foreign objects in food/drink, spoiled or expired food, customer illness after consuming food, or harassment/unsafe behaviour. Escalate immediately to Store Manager or Operations Manager.

qa_pairs.instruction: What should I do if a customer reports an allergen contamination?
qa_pairs.output: This is a Level 3 Safety & Compliance Concern. Escalate immediately to the Store Manager or Operations Manager. Follow incident reporting procedures. Do not try to handle this independently.

qa_pairs.instruction: How should I handle a wrong item served to a customer?
qa_pairs.output: This is a Level 2 complaint. Apologize, correct the mistake immediately, remake or replace the item, and inform the shift lead if needed. A partial refund can be processed by Shift Manager and above.

chunks.title: Absolute NOs — Things to Never Do
chunks.intent: List the absolute behaviors that must never happen in customer interactions.
chunks.content: Absolute NOs at TWC/TR: Never delay or miss a greeting due to distractions like phones or internal conversations. Never invade a customer's personal space or touch children without permission. There's no place for rude behavior, impatience, or negative body language in customer interactions. Never discriminate against customers based on caste, creed, gender, religion, age, appearance, or ability. Avoid loud internal conversations or discussing operational matters where customers can hear them. Never ignore customers during ordering or service interactions. Don't argue with customers or prioritize internal tasks over customer service. Avoid approaching customers for feedback while they are on calls, engaged in conversation, or visibly upset. Never pressure customers to give feedback, post reviews, or bring more people. Don't overlook cleanliness, hygiene, or maintenance issues anywhere in the café. If an issue can be resolved during the dine-in experience without escalation, don't provide the customer care contact details instead.

qa_pairs.instruction: What are the absolute NOs at TWC?
qa_pairs.output: Never miss greetings due to distractions. Never invade personal space or touch children without permission. No rude behavior or negative body language. Never discriminate. Avoid loud operational discussions near customers. Don't ignore customers during service. Don't argue with customers. Don't pressure customers for reviews. Don't overlook cleanliness issues.

qa_pairs.instruction: Can I approach a customer for feedback while they're on a call?
qa_pairs.output: No. Avoid approaching customers for feedback while they are on calls, engaged in conversation, or visibly upset. Wait until they are comfortable.

qa_pairs.instruction: Should I give the customer care number if I can resolve the issue?
qa_pairs.output: No. If an issue can be resolved during the dine-in experience without escalation, resolve it yourself. Don't provide customer care contact details instead of solving the problem.

qa_pairs.instruction: Can I pressure a customer to post a review?
qa_pairs.output: No. Never pressure customers to give feedback, post reviews, or bring more people. This is an Absolute NO at TWC/TR.

chunks.title: Coffee Pro Tips
chunks.intent: Key pro tips for delivering excellent C.O.F.F.E.E. experience.
chunks.content: Pro Tips for CX at TWC/TR: Ensure your tone matches your words. A 'Welcome to Third Wave/Third Rush' with a cheerful tone lets the customer know you are happy to have them. Saying it in a dull manner with no enthusiasm makes the customer feel unwelcome and uneasy. A warm smile, eye contact, and a friendly greeting within a respectful 2-meter distance set the tone for the entire customer experience. Speak clearly, maintain positive body language, and keep your tone warm and professional in every interaction. Anticipate customer needs early. A small gesture like guiding to a seat or opening the door creates a welcoming start. Help customers make confident choices by guiding them through the menu and confirming their order and customizations before serving. A well-groomed and hygienic appearance reinforces trust and professionalism from the very first interaction. Master salver management: never over-stack the salver, hold it while serving instead of placing it on the table, and when empty, carry it neatly under the arm. Present orders with confidence. Announce the items clearly and serve with a genuine smile to enhance the experience. Choose the right moment for feedback. Approach customers once they are comfortable and have experienced the product. Let confident body language (standing upright, walking with a calm steady pace, avoiding hunched shoulders) reflect your knowledge and reassure customers they are in capable hands. End every visit on a high note. Make eye contact, smile, and thank customers enthusiastically as they leave.

qa_pairs.instruction: What are the CX pro tips for baristas?
qa_pairs.output: Key pro tips: Match your tone to your words. Smile, make eye contact, and greet within 2 meters. Speak clearly with positive body language. Anticipate needs early. Guide through menu. Maintain a groomed, hygienic appearance. Master salver management. Present orders with confidence. Choose the right moment for feedback. Let confident body language reflect your knowledge. End every visit with a thank you.

qa_pairs.instruction: What is proper salver management?
qa_pairs.output: Never over-stack the salver. Hold it while serving instead of placing it on the table. When empty, carry it neatly under the arm.

qa_pairs.instruction: When is the right time to approach a customer for feedback?
qa_pairs.output: Approach once they are comfortable and have experienced the product — typically after they've consumed about one-third of it. Don't approach while they're on calls, in conversation, or visibly upset.

chunks.title: Social Media Etiquette
chunks.intent: What can and cannot be captured and posted on social media representing the café.
chunks.content: Social media etiquette for TWC café content. Hygiene: CAN capture — wearing gloves while making/garnishing beverages, handling food, touching ready-to-serve items, clean counters. MUST NOT capture — bare-hand contact, unclean counter/equipment, phone near food areas, spills, mess, or eating while preparing orders. Setup and Presentation: CAN capture — full uniform and neat appearance, organized stations. MUST NOT capture — incomplete uniform or untidy look, casual clothes inside café, cluttered setup or dull/empty café. Content: CAN capture — coffee making, latte art, café ambience, menu items, team moments (professional behavior only), happy customer moments (with permission). MUST NOT capture — inside kitchen/storage, billing screens, cash handling or customer data, staff conflicts, operational issues, empty café shown negatively. Product: CAN capture — products looking fresh and neat, correct cup sizes, logo visible. MUST NOT capture — unfinished drinks or rejected orders, incorrectly named products, messy tables. Communication: Use correct brand name 'Third Wave Coffee/Third Rush', correct product names, clear speech. MUST NOT — wrong pronunciation, wrong product names, misleading info or unverified offers. Audio: CAN use — soft music, clean original audio. MUST NOT — loud or offensive audio, songs in languages other than English. Brand: CAN post — clean, premium, customer-ready content. MUST NOT — anything that harms trust or image. 5·second check before posting: 1. Am I in proper uniform? 2. Am I wearing gloves while preparing orders? 3. Is the café clean? 4. Does the product look perfect? If not, fix first. 5. Does this make the brand look good?

qa_pairs.instruction: What social media rules should café staff follow?
qa_pairs.output: Only post clean, professional, brand-positive content. Show proper uniform, gloves while preparing, clean counters, and fresh products. Never post bare-hand food contact, messy tables, kitchen/storage areas, billing screens, customer data, or staff conflicts. Use correct brand names and product names. Do a 5-second check before posting.

qa_pairs.instruction: Can I post a video of latte art on social media?
qa_pairs.output: Yes, coffee making and latte art can be captured for social media, as long as hygiene standards are visible (wearing gloves), the setup is clean, and correct brand names are used.

qa_pairs.instruction: What is the 5-second check before posting café content?
qa_pairs.output: Before posting, check: 1. Am I in proper uniform? 2. Am I wearing gloves while preparing orders? 3. Is the café clean? 4. Does the product look perfect? If not, fix first. 5. Does this make the brand look good?

qa_pairs.instruction: Can I post customer photos on social media?
qa_pairs.output: Happy customer moments can be captured only WITH permission. Never post customer data, billing screens, or anything that compromises customer privacy.

chunks.title: Telephone Etiquette
chunks.intent: How to handle phone calls professionally representing the café.
chunks.content: Telephone etiquette at TWC/TR. Best Practices: Start with 'Hi, thank you for calling Third Wave/Third Rush.' Introduce yourself and ask how you can help. Maintain a calm, kind, and polite tone. Inform clearly if an item is out of stock and offer an equal or better alternative including pricing. Prioritize in-store customers appropriately — if on call, ensure another team member assists the in-store customer. Stay fully present during the call. Return all missed calls. End the call politely. Keep communication clear and realistic. Avoid: Don't skip the greeting or sound abrupt. Don't jump into conversation without context. Don't sound rude, impatient, or distracted. Don't just say 'not available' without offering alternatives. Don't leave a customer hanging while on call. Don't multitask (POS, order prep, etc.) while on call. Don't ignore missed calls. Don't hang up mid-conversation. Don't make commitments or promises over the phone. Role-based phone handling: Basic Barista — handle queries (menu, recommendations, availability). Advanced Barista / Buddy Trainer — handle order issues (wrong/missing items, delays, taste concerns), but don't commit to refunds/replacements without approval. Shift Manager & above — handle escalations (unhappy customers, repeated issues, resolution requests), don't delay escalation in sensitive situations. ASM / Store Manager — handle serious concerns (refund demands, safety, health, hygiene issues), don't delegate high-risk situations downward.

qa_pairs.instruction: How should I answer the phone at the café?
qa_pairs.output: Start with: 'Hi, thank you for calling Third Wave/Third Rush.' Introduce yourself and ask how you can help. Maintain a calm, kind, and polite tone throughout.

qa_pairs.instruction: What should I do if a customer calls and an item is out of stock?
qa_pairs.output: Inform clearly that the item is out of stock and offer an equal or better alternative, including pricing. Never just say 'not available' without offering alternatives.

qa_pairs.instruction: Should I return missed calls at the café?
qa_pairs.output: Yes, return all missed calls. Every call is part of the customer experience and ignoring missed calls is not acceptable.

qa_pairs.instruction: Who can handle phone complaints about refunds?
qa_pairs.output: ASM or Store Manager handles serious concerns including refund demands. Shift Managers handle escalations. Advanced Baristas and Buddy Trainers can handle order issues but must not commit to refunds/replacements without approval.

qa_pairs.instruction: Can I multitask while on a customer call?
qa_pairs.output: No. Stay fully present during the call. Don't multitask with POS, order prep, etc. while on the call.

chunks.title: TWC App & Loyalty Program
chunks.intent: Explain what the TWC app offers and the loyalty program structure.
chunks.content: The TWC App allows customers to: Order for Dine-In (order while at the café), Order for Takeaway (skip the queue), Pay for orders using the app wallet or other payment options, Track loyalty progress and rewards, Pre-buy Packs to get discounted items and redeem them before expiry. Brew Journey loyalty program: After 5th order of Rs 300+, customer makes 1st order (minimum order value) and gets 3 Free Classic Coffee cups with 15-day validity plus a 10% OFF coupon. After 2nd order of Rs 300+, customer gets 20% OFF coupon with 15-day validity. Orders 2–5 must be Rs 300+ each, with 5 qualifying orders within 45 days to complete the brew journey. Unclaimed rewards expire after 45 days. Loyalty tiers: Partner (default tier, Rs 100 OFF as signing up bonus + 1 Free Coffee Cup), Influencer (6 successful orders, 10% Wave Coins, online-only payable, +2 Free Coffee Cups), Ambassador (12 successful orders, 15% Wave Coins, online-only payable). Cycle is 90 days — maintain 6 orders (Influencer) or 12 orders (Ambassador) to avoid downgrade. Only one discount (cup, coin, pack, etc.) can be used per order.

qa_pairs.instruction: What can customers do on the TWC app?
qa_pairs.output: Customers can order for dine-in, order for takeaway (skip the queue), pay using the app wallet or other options, track loyalty progress and rewards, and pre-buy packs for discounted items.

qa_pairs.instruction: How does the Brew Journey loyalty program work?
qa_pairs.output: After the 5th order of Rs 300+, customers get 3 Free Classic Coffee cups (15-day validity) and a 10% OFF coupon. After the 2nd order (Rs 300+), they get 20% OFF (15-day validity). Five qualifying orders of Rs 300+ within 45 days complete the journey. Unclaimed rewards expire after 45 days.

qa_pairs.instruction: What are the loyalty tiers on the TWC app?
qa_pairs.output: Three tiers: Partner (default — Rs 100 OFF + 1 Free Coffee), Influencer (6 orders — 10% Wave Coins + 2 Free Coffees), Ambassador (12 orders — 15% Wave Coins). Maintain order counts within 90-day cycles to keep your tier.

qa_pairs.instruction: Can a customer use multiple discounts on one order?
qa_pairs.output: No, only one discount (cup, coin, pack, etc.) can be used per order.

qa_pairs.instruction: What are Wave Coins?
qa_pairs.output: Wave Coins are loyalty rewards in the TWC app. Influencer tier members earn 10% Wave Coins and Ambassador tier members earn 15% Wave Coins. They can be used for payment (online only).

chunks.title: Journey from Good to Amazing
chunks.intent: Explain the three levels of service quality: Consistency, Going Above & Beyond, and Beyond.
chunks.content: The CX Playbook defines the journey from good to amazing in three levels. Consistency: Same taste, same quality, every time. SOP adherence. Cleanliness and ambiance maintained daily. Reliable service standards. Consistency builds trust. Going Above & Beyond: Surprise with a thoughtful effort. Suggest pairing (coffee + dessert). Offer proactive help. Personalize recommendations. Eye contact, authentic smile. Beyond: Anticipate customer needs. Going beyond builds delight. The emotional differentiator. Impact on Customers: They can expect a premium standard of service. They develop trust and prioritize their needs. They notice us going above and beyond to help. Outcomes: Improvement in CPI (Customer Pulse Index), decrease in complaints, improved brand association. Impact on Teams: Upholding TWC standards through quality of coffee, beverage, food, desserts, and service. Motivation from making customers happy. Clear direction for customer experience. Outcomes: Employee pride, skilled people, internal growth.

qa_pairs.instruction: What is the journey from good to amazing at TWC?
qa_pairs.output: Three levels: 1. Consistency — same taste, quality, SOP adherence, reliable standards. 2. Going Above & Beyond — surprise with thoughtful effort, suggest pairings, proactive help, personalized recommendations. 3. Beyond — anticipate customer needs, the emotional differentiator.

qa_pairs.instruction: What is the Customer Pulse Index (CPI)?
qa_pairs.output: The CPI measures customer satisfaction. Out of every 1,000 customers served, it tracks how many were unhappy or had complaints. A smaller CPI number means better service. Improving CPI is an outcome of consistent, above-and-beyond customer experience.

qa_pairs.instruction: What does consistency mean in CX at TWC?
qa_pairs.output: Consistency means same taste, same quality, every time. It includes SOP adherence, maintaining cleanliness and ambiance daily, and reliable service standards. Consistency builds customer trust.

qa_pairs.instruction: How does great CX impact the business?
qa_pairs.output: Great CX leads to: improvement in CPI (Customer Pulse Index), decrease in complaints, improved brand association, employee pride, skilled workforce, and internal growth.

chunks.title: The Third Way — Place, People, Product, Process
chunks.intent: TWC's four pillars that define the brand identity.
chunks.content: The Third Way defines how TWC and Third Rush operate, built on four pillars. Place: We offer a clean, well-lit, and thoughtfully arranged space with comfortable seating and well-maintained elements that create a cozy, welcoming atmosphere. People: Our team is highly trained and understands customer experience deeply — not just as a process, but as something we genuinely believe in. Product: We serve specialty coffee crafted with care and precision, along with coffee beans and merchandise for customers to enjoy. We go the extra mile. Process: We say 'absolutely' wherever possible to show customers we are intentional in our care.

qa_pairs.instruction: What is The Third Way?
qa_pairs.output: The Third Way defines how TWC and Third Rush operate, built on four pillars: Place (clean, cozy, welcoming space), People (highly trained team that genuinely believes in CX), Product (specialty coffee crafted with care and precision), and Process (saying 'absolutely' and being intentional in care).

qa_pairs.instruction: What are the four pillars of TWC's brand identity?
qa_pairs.output: Place — welcoming, well-maintained space. People — trained team that understands CX deeply. Product — specialty coffee crafted with care. Process — intentional care, saying 'absolutely' wherever possible.

chunks.title: Coffee Tastings & Product Samplings
chunks.intent: Explain the value and impact of coffee tastings and product samplings in the café.
chunks.content: Coffee tastings and product samplings enhance the customer experience while strengthening trust, engagement, and sales. Showcases Barista Knowledge & Craft: Coffee tastings allow baristas to share their expertise by highlighting flavour notes, origins, and brewing methods. Highlights barista skill and knowledge. Helps customers better appreciate coffee. Shows customers that they are in good hands with experts. Builds Customer Trust: Offering tastings shows confidence in product quality and transparency in the customer experience. Reinforces trust in the brand. Makes customers feel valued. Builds confidence in product quality. Creates a memorable experience and builds connection. Makes customers feel appreciated. Boosts Sales: Allowing customers to try a product reduces hesitation and encourages them to order something new. Encourages customers to try unfamiliar or new items. Increases likelihood of purchase. Promotes repeat orders for products they enjoy. Builds long-term customer loyalty. Sets the base for suggestive and upselling. For more details, see Wisdom Wednesday — Back to Basics: Driving Coffee Culture.

qa_pairs.instruction: Why are coffee tastings important at TWC?
qa_pairs.output: Coffee tastings showcase barista knowledge and craft, build customer trust by showing confidence in product quality, and boost sales by reducing hesitation about new items. They create memorable experiences and build long-term customer loyalty.

qa_pairs.instruction: How do product samplings help with sales?
qa_pairs.output: Samplings allow customers to try products, reducing hesitation about ordering something new. This encourages trying unfamiliar items, increases purchase likelihood, promotes repeat orders, and sets the base for suggestive selling and upselling.

qa_pairs.instruction: What does a barista showcase during a coffee tasting?
qa_pairs.output: Baristas share expertise by highlighting flavour notes, origins, and brewing methods. This helps customers better appreciate coffee  and shows them they're in good hands with experts.

modules.title: WING's — IDP Process & Bench Planning (Detailed)
modules.description: Detailed guide to the WING's Internal Development Process (IDP) and Bench Planning: the five-step IDP flow, employee journey from Barista to Area Manager, interview panel composition, training periods, and certification requirements.

chunks.title: WING's Acronym & Program Purpose
chunks.intent: Explain what WING's stands for and the program's objectives.
chunks.content: WING's stands for: W — Winning, I — Integrated Development, N — Nurturing Talent, G — Getting Ready for Success. The program's objectives are: Build a strong internal talent pipeline by identifying and developing high-potential employees. Ensure trained and promotion-ready team members are available to fill key vacancies. Reduce business disruption caused by sudden resignations, lack of trained replacements, and reliance on underperformers. Enable proactive hiring and development rather than reactive staffing decisions. Avoid loss of business opportunities due to manpower gaps. Support consistent café performance by strengthening the link between people capability, customer satisfaction, and business profitability. The program implements two key processes: Internal Development Process (IDP) and Bench Planning.

qa_pairs.instruction: What does WING's stand for?
qa_pairs.output: WING's stands for W — Winning, I — Integrated Development, N — Nurturing Talent, G — Getting Ready for Success.

qa_pairs.instruction: What is the full form of WING's?
qa_pairs.output: WING's stands for Winning, Integrated Development, Nurturing Talent, Getting Ready for Success.

qa_pairs.instruction: What are the objectives of the WING's program?
qa_pairs.output: The objectives are: build a strong internal talent pipeline, ensure trained and promotion-ready team members are available for key vacancies, reduce business disruption from sudden resignations or lack of trained replacements, enable proactive hiring and development, avoid loss of business opportunities due to manpower gaps, and support consistent café performance by linking people capability, customer satisfaction, and business profitability.

qa_pairs.instruction: What two key processes does WING's implement?
qa_pairs.output: WING's implements two key processes: Internal Development Process (IDP) and Bench Planning.

qa_pairs.instruction: Why is the WING's program important?
qa_pairs.output: It prevents business disruption from sudden resignations, ensures trained replacements are ready, enables proactive development instead of reactive staffing, and strengthens the link between people capability, customer satisfaction, and profitability.

chunks.title: The Five-Step IDP Process
chunks.intent: Explain the five steps of the Internal Development Process in detail.
chunks.content: The IDP process has five steps: Step 1 — Identification: Match the eligibility criteria. Auto-selection happens through Zing based on tenure and mandate certifications. Step 2 — Readiness: Check readiness by filling the Readiness Checklist. This is done by the reporting manager. Step 3 — Assessment: Assess the candidate through an assessment centre. This is conducted by the Regional Trainer. Step 4 — Interview: Conduct the interview following the interview questionnaire based on RESPECT. This is conducted by the panel. Step 5 — Training: On clearing the interview with the panel, the candidate will start training for the next position.

qa_pairs.instruction: What are the five steps of the IDP process?
qa_pairs.output: The five steps are: (1) Identification — match eligibility criteria via Zing based on tenure and certifications, (2) Readiness — fill the Readiness Checklist done by the reporting manager, (3) Assessment — assessment centre conducted by the Regional Trainer, (4) Interview — conducted by a panel using RESPECT-based questionnaire, (5) Training — start training for the next position after clearing the interview.

qa_pairs.instruction: How does identification work in IDP?
qa_pairs.output: Identification is auto-selection through Zing. It checks eligibility based on tenure and mandate certifications.

qa_pairs.instruction: Who conducts the readiness check in IDP?
qa_pairs.output: The readiness check is done by the reporting manager using a Readiness Checklist.

qa_pairs.instruction: Who conducts the IDP assessment?
qa_pairs.output: The assessment is conducted by the Regional Trainer through an assessment centre.

qa_pairs.instruction: What happens after clearing the IDP interview?
qa_pairs.output: After clearing the interview with the panel, the candidate starts training for the next position.

qa_pairs.instruction: What is the IDP interview based on?
qa_pairs.output: The IDP interview follows a questionnaire based on the RESPECT framework.

chunks.title: Employee Journey in WING's
chunks.intent: Describe the full employee journey from Barista to Store Manager through the WING's program.
chunks.content: The employee journey through WING's follows this path: Barista (3 months) → eligible to be Buddy Trainer (BT). BT certification includes a BT Workshop and BT Skill Check. BT (6 months) → eligible to become Shift Manager. Requires Readiness Checklist for BT, Aptitude & Attitude test, Panel Interview & Selection. Shift Manager → eligible to become ASM (9 months). Includes Practice Learning, Basic Certification (15 days), Food Safety Module Review, Advance Certification, Apply the Learning, Final Certification, SHLP Workshop, Readiness Checklist, Aptitude & Attitude test, Panel Interview & Selection. ASM → eligible to become Store Manager (SM). Each stage requires Readiness Checklist completion, assessment, and panel interview clearance.

qa_pairs.instruction: What is the employee journey in the WING's program?
qa_pairs.output: Barista (3 months) → Buddy Trainer → Shift Manager (6 months tenure) → ASM (9 months) → Store Manager. Each transition requires a Readiness Checklist, assessment, and panel interview.

qa_pairs.instruction: How long before a Barista can become a Buddy Trainer?
qa_pairs.output: A Barista is eligible to become a Buddy Trainer after 3 months.

qa_pairs.instruction: How long before a BT can become a Shift Manager?
qa_pairs.output: A Buddy Trainer is eligible to become a Shift Manager after 6 months tenure. They must complete a Readiness Checklist, Aptitude & Attitude test, and Panel Interview.

qa_pairs.instruction: What certifications are needed for ASM?
qa_pairs.output: To become ASM, a Shift Manager needs: Practice Learning, Basic Certification (15 days), Food Safety Module Review, Advance Certification, Final Certification, and SHLP Workshop, followed by Readiness Checklist, Aptitude & Attitude test, and Panel Interview.

qa_pairs.instruction: What is the promotion path at TWC?
qa_pairs.output: Barista → Buddy Trainer (3 months) → Shift Manager (6 months) → ASM (9 months) → Store Manager. Each step requires readiness assessment, aptitude test, and panel interview clearance.

chunks.title: Interview Panel Composition
chunks.intent: Define who sits on the interview panel for each promotion level.
chunks.content: Interview panel composition by promotion level: Buddy Trainer to Shift Manager: Area Manager, Trainer/Regional QA, HRBP. Shift Manager to ASM: Market Manager/Regional Manager, Regional Trainer/QA, Regional HRBP. ASM to Store Manager: Regional Manager, GM Operations, Training Head/QA Head, Regional HRBP. Area Manager/Cross-function: Director — Ops & Excellence, GM Operations/Cross-function Head, Finance Representative, Training Head, HR Head. The candidate gets a maximum of two opportunities to qualify. If they fail the first round, they must complete the development plan and be reassessed after 30 days. If they fail the second round, they are eligible for IDP again after 3 months from the second interview date.

qa_pairs.instruction: Who is on the interview panel for Buddy Trainer to Shift Manager?
qa_pairs.output: The panel includes the Area Manager, Trainer/Regional QA, and HRBP.

qa_pairs.instruction: Who is on the interview panel for Shift Manager to ASM?
qa_pairs.output: The panel includes the Market Manager/Regional Manager, Regional Trainer/QA, and Regional HRBP.

qa_pairs.instruction: Who is on the interview panel for ASM to Store Manager?
qa_pairs.output: The panel includes the Regional Manager, GM Operations, Training Head/QA Head, and Regional HRBP.

qa_pairs.instruction: What happens if a candidate fails the IDP interview?
qa_pairs.output: The candidate gets a maximum of two attempts. If they fail the first round, they must complete a development plan and be reassessed after 30 days. If they fail the second round, they become eligible again after 3 months from the second interview date.

qa_pairs.instruction: How many chances does a candidate get in the IDP interview?
qa_pairs.output: A maximum of two opportunities. After the first failure, reassessment after 30 days with a completed development plan. After the second failure, eligible again after 3 months.

chunks.title: Training Periods & Certifications by Role
chunks.intent: Detail the training period and review process for each role transition.
chunks.content: Training periods and certifications by promotion level: Barista to Shift Manager: Training period 30 days, in-store review weekly by Reporting Manager, final reviews and certifications by Trainer & SM/AM. Shift Manager to ASM: Training period 45 days, in-store review weekly by Store Manager, reviews and certifications by Trainer & SM/AM. ASM to Store Manager: Training period 15 days, in-store review weekly by Store Manager/AM, reviews and certifications by Trainer & AM. Area Manager/Cross-function: Training period 30 days, on-field review weekly by Reporting Manager, final review and certification by Training & Ops Head.

qa_pairs.instruction: What is the training period for Barista to Shift Manager?
qa_pairs.output: 30 days. In-store review is weekly by the Reporting Manager. Final reviews and certifications are by the Trainer & SM/AM.

qa_pairs.instruction: What is the training period for Shift Manager to ASM?
qa_pairs.output: 45 days. In-store review is weekly by the Store Manager. Reviews and certifications by Trainer & SM/AM.

qa_pairs.instruction: What is the training period for ASM to Store Manager?
qa_pairs.output: 15 days. In-store review is weekly by the Store Manager/AM. Reviews and certifications by Trainer & AM.

qa_pairs.instruction: What is the training period for Area Manager?
qa_pairs.output: 30 days. On-field review is weekly by the Reporting Manager. Final review and certification by Training & Ops Head.

qa_pairs.instruction: How long is each training period in WING's?
qa_pairs.output: Barista to Shift Manager: 30 days. Shift Manager to ASM: 45 days. ASM to Store Manager: 15 days. Area Manager/Cross-function: 30 days.

modules.title: RESPECT Framework — Role-Specific Behaviors
modules.description: Detailed RESPECT framework behaviors expected from Baristas, Shift Managers, and Store Managers for each value: Responsibility, Empathy, Service Excellence, Performance with Purpose, Ethics & Integrity, Collaboration, and Trust.

chunks.title: RESPECT Full Form & Overview
chunks.intent: Explain what RESPECT stands for and provide the full form.
chunks.content: RESPECT is the core values framework at Third Wave Coffee. The full form of RESPECT is: R — Responsibility, E — Empathy, S — Service Excellence, P — Performance with Purpose, E — Ethics & Integrity, C — Collaboration, T — Trust. Each value has specific behavioral expectations for Baristas, Shift Managers, and Store Managers. RESPECT guides all employee behavior and decision-making at every level of the organization.

qa_pairs.instruction: What is the full form of RESPECT?
qa_pairs.output: R — Responsibility, E — Empathy, S — Service Excellence, P — Performance with Purpose, E — Ethics & Integrity, C — Collaboration, T — Trust.

qa_pairs.instruction: What does R.E.S.P.E.C.T. stand for?
qa_pairs.output: R.E.S.P.E.C.T. stands for Responsibility, Empathy, Service Excellence, Performance with Purpose, Ethics & Integrity, Collaboration, and Trust.

qa_pairs.instruction: What is the RESPECT framework?
qa_pairs.output: RESPECT is the core values framework at Third Wave Coffee. It stands for Responsibility, Empathy, Service Excellence, Performance with Purpose, Ethics & Integrity, Collaboration, and Trust. Each value has specific behavioral expectations for Baristas, Shift Managers, and Store Managers.

qa_pairs.instruction: What are the seven RESPECT values?
qa_pairs.output: The seven RESPECT values are: Responsibility, Empathy, Service Excellence, Performance with Purpose, Ethics & Integrity, Collaboration, and Trust.

qa_pairs.instruction: How many values does RESPECT have?
qa_pairs.output: RESPECT has seven values: Responsibility, Empathy, Service Excellence, Performance with Purpose, Ethics & Integrity, Collaboration, and Trust.

chunks.title: Responsibility — Role-Specific Behaviors
chunks.intent: Detail what Responsibility means for each role level.
chunks.content: Responsibility: 'We take ownership of our actions, honour commitments, and uphold the highest standards of excellence in all that we do.' Barista responsibilities: Report at store as per schedule. Follow grooming standards. Maintain personal hygiene and follow CAYG while working on stations. Follow recipe and TWC standards to prepare and serve products. Take personal accountability to own the experience of customers and partners in the café. Shift Manager responsibilities: Divide work load by following the deployment. Take care of people on shift by providing breaks on time. Take accountability that all policies, standards, and procedures are followed. Be responsible to share honest feedback with the team to help them improve. Store Manager responsibilities: Ensure duty roster is prepared at least three days before the deployment date. Take responsibility that no one is working consecutively for 10 days without weekly off. Take ownership of actions and decisions. Take responsibility of café performance and take appropriate actions to improve. Take sustainability and social responsibility initiatives involving store teams. Don'ts: Making excuses or avoiding responsibility. Withholding information that affects the team or customers. Ignoring behaviors and actions that impact partners, customers, and business negatively. Don't waste resources (water, electricity, packaging material); adopt a mindset of shared responsibility.

qa_pairs.instruction: What does Responsibility mean in RESPECT?
qa_pairs.output: Responsibility means taking ownership of actions, honouring commitments, and upholding the highest standards of excellence. It covers respect for each other (being dependable and supportive), respect for process and SOPs, and respect for customer trust.

qa_pairs.instruction: What are the Barista responsibilities under RESPECT?
qa_pairs.output: Report as per schedule, follow grooming standards, maintain personal hygiene and CAYG, follow recipe and TWC standards, and take personal accountability for the experience of customers and partners.

qa_pairs.instruction: What are the Shift Manager responsibilities under RESPECT?
qa_pairs.output: Divide workload following deployment, provide breaks on time, ensure all policies and procedures are followed, and share honest feedback with the team to help them improve.

qa_pairs.instruction: What are the Store Manager responsibilities under RESPECT?
qa_pairs.output: Prepare duty roster at least 3 days before deployment, ensure no one works 10 consecutive days without weekly off, take ownership of decisions, take responsibility for café performance, and lead sustainability initiatives involving store teams.

qa_pairs.instruction: What are the don'ts for Responsibility?
qa_pairs.output: Don't make excuses or avoid responsibility. Don't withhold information affecting team or customers. Don't ignore negative behaviors impacting partners, customers, or business. Don't waste resources like water, electricity, or packaging material.

chunks.title: Empathy — Role-Specific Behaviors
chunks.intent: Detail what Empathy means for each role level.
chunks.content: Empathy: 'We foster an inclusive environment where diverse perspectives are valued, and meaningful connections drive collaboration and innovation.' Barista: Welcome every team member with warmth. Help customers who may struggle (e.g., opening door, finding comfortable seat). Approach customers who appear upset or unhappy with care. Patiently teach skills multiple times to new team members. If a new partner struggles with a task, demonstrate an easier way. Shift Manager: Provide feedback patiently and repeatedly to help improve behaviors. Demonstrate difficult skills while ensuring partners feel supported. Understand that mistakes happen and avoid scolding; work collaboratively to find solutions. Appreciate and acknowledge the team's empathetic behavior. Store Manager: Accommodate scheduling requests when possible to support work-life balance. Foster an inclusive and welcoming culture where both partners and customers feel valued, respected, and included. Care for customers and partners with special needs. Accept feedback openly, even from equal level or junior employees. Empower the team to take empathetic actions (e.g., offering a coffee tasting to a lonely customer, celebrating a customer's birthday). Don'ts: Don't judge others. Don't be egoistic or uncomfortable receiving feedback from peers. Never ignore burnout signs. Don't dismiss feedback. Don't be unapproachable.

qa_pairs.instruction: What does Empathy mean in RESPECT?
qa_pairs.output: Empathy means fostering an inclusive environment where diverse perspectives are valued and meaningful connections drive collaboration and innovation. It includes respect for diversity, customer needs, and engagement.

qa_pairs.instruction: How should a Barista show empathy?
qa_pairs.output: Welcome team members warmly, help struggling customers (open doors, find seats), approach upset customers with care, patiently teach new team members, and demonstrate easier ways to do tasks.

qa_pairs.instruction: How should a Shift Manager show empathy?
qa_pairs.output: Provide feedback patiently and repeatedly, demonstrate difficult skills while keeping partners supported, avoid scolding for mistakes — collaborate on solutions, and appreciate the team's empathetic behavior.

qa_pairs.instruction: How should a Store Manager show empathy?
qa_pairs.output: Accommodate scheduling requests for work-life balance, foster inclusive culture, care for people with special needs, accept feedback from any level, and empower the team to take empathetic actions like celebrating a customer's birthday.

qa_pairs.instruction: What are the don'ts for Empathy?
qa_pairs.output: Don't judge others. Don't be egoistic about receiving feedback from peers. Never ignore burnout signs. Don't dismiss feedback. Don't be unapproachable.

chunks.title: Service Excellence — Role-Specific Behaviors
chunks.intent: Detail what Service Excellence means for each role level.
chunks.content: Service Excellence: 'We are relentless in delivering outstanding experiences, setting high standards, and continuously improving to exceed expectations.' Barista: Engage with customers and gather feedback on food and beverages. Remember customer names and preferred drinks. Maintain efficiency at station, stay hospitable, smile, and greet customers. Adhere to standards and recipes even during peak hours. Understand customer preferences and recommend products accordingly. Handle difficult or unhappy customers calmly and respectfully. Shift Manager: Monitor customer satisfaction and help team view the café from a customer's perspective. Ensure clear communication and all shift tasks are completed. Encourage product purchases through customer engagement. Act as a role model in resolving customer concerns. Store Manager: Be a role model, encourage café team to connect with customers by creating a recognition culture. Plan operational activities during non-peak hours to prioritize customers during peak hours. Consistently follow up to ensure store is clean and ready for service excellence. Adopt and motivate team to adopt new ways of working. Don'ts: Never prioritize cost control at the expense of customer experience. Never prioritize operational tasks over customers. Never ignore customer concerns or special requests. Never prove a point or assume customers are seeking free products.

qa_pairs.instruction: What does Service Excellence mean in RESPECT?
qa_pairs.output: Service Excellence means being relentless in delivering outstanding experiences, setting high standards, and continuously improving to exceed expectations.

qa_pairs.instruction: How should a Barista practice Service Excellence?
qa_pairs.output: Engage with customers and gather feedback, remember customer names and preferred drinks, maintain efficiency and hospitality, adhere to standards even during peak hours, recommend products based on preferences, and handle difficult customers calmly.

qa_pairs.instruction: How should a Store Manager practice Service Excellence?
qa_pairs.output: Be a role model creating a recognition culture, plan operational activities during non-peak hours to prioritize customers during peak hours, ensure store cleanliness and readiness, and motivate team to adopt new ways of working.

qa_pairs.instruction: What are the don'ts for Service Excellence?
qa_pairs.output: Never prioritize cost control over customer experience. Never prioritize operational tasks over customers. Never ignore concerns or special requests. Never assume customers are seeking free products.

chunks.title: Performance with Purpose — Role-Specific Behaviors
chunks.intent: Detail what Performance with Purpose means for each role level.
chunks.content: Performance with Purpose: 'We are driven by a results-oriented mindset, where excellence, growth, and ethical leadership define success.' Barista: Complete all assigned tasks efficiently and timely. Demonstrate excellent suggestive and upselling skills. Understand why it's important to follow standards and policies. Follow all standards and guidelines all the time and inspire others. Stay curious and eager to learn new skills to deliver best results. Break bigger goals into smaller actions. Shift Manager: Keep a firm and professional approach to demand results. Adjust delivery style — tone, pace — to fit the message and audience. Set and maintain high standards by following company policies and procedures. Implement innovative ideas to achieve results. Develop new skills to deliver results. Believe in everyone. Store Manager: Communicate effectively, set expectations, monitor and coach team. Involve, motivate, and guide team to achieve results. Balance people, customer, and business results. Approach team on how small things like preparing best quality coffee, product availability, maintaining SOS, good VM, and customer connection at all touchpoints help drive sales and results. Recognize top performers based on objective performance metrics. Don'ts: Never approach aggressively and demand results. Don't perceive tasks as burden. Don't withhold financial goals from team. Never be rigid to adapt newer ways. Never discriminate based on caste, colour, sex, or ethnicity.

qa_pairs.instruction: What does Performance with Purpose mean in RESPECT?
qa_pairs.output: Performance with Purpose means being driven by a results-oriented mindset where excellence, growth, and ethical leadership define success.

qa_pairs.instruction: How should a Barista show Performance with Purpose?
qa_pairs.output: Complete tasks efficiently, demonstrate excellent suggestive and upselling skills, follow standards consistently, stay curious to learn, and break bigger goals into smaller actions.

qa_pairs.instruction: How should a Store Manager show Performance with Purpose?
qa_pairs.output: Communicate effectively and coach team, involve and motivate team to achieve results, balance people/customer/business results, show how small things drive sales, and recognize top performers based on objective metrics.

qa_pairs.instruction: What are the don'ts for Performance with Purpose?
qa_pairs.output: Never demand results aggressively. Don't treat tasks as a burden. Don't withhold financial goals from team. Don't be rigid about adopting new ways. Never discriminate based on caste, colour, sex, or ethnicity.

chunks.title: Ethics & Integrity — Role-Specific Behaviors
chunks.intent: Detail what Ethics & Integrity means for each role level.
chunks.content: Ethics & Integrity: 'We build trust through honesty, transparency, and ethical decision-making in every aspect of our business.' Barista: Always disclose important information which HR needs to know like relationships with vendors, partners, own health conditions. Do correct entries in the POS. Follow HR policies. Report if any mishappening is witnessed. Shift Manager: Consistently observe people while working and correct them when required. Effectively handle unexpected circumstances and incidents following Third Wave standards and policies. Reflect Third Wave transparency in every transaction. Store Manager: Provide equal opportunities to all employees to grow. Others consider him/her the most trustworthy person. Keep confidential information and pass on only required information to the team. Abide by law, ask if unaware. Share company information only with authorized persons. Observe, involve to ensure no mishappening in the store, report or take action if anything observed. Don'ts: Never do unlawful activities at the store (consuming drugs, alcohol, tobacco at workplace, carrying unlawful substances, reselling TWC products, mismanagement of compliance, workplace harassment, physical violence, POSH violations). Never accept gifts from vendors/customers worth more than Rs. 500. Never take unauthorized benefits (e.g., giving employee meal/discount to friends or customers). Never manipulate or hide information (incorrect inventories, wrong POS entries, false statements). Never submit fraudulent claims (forging attendance, overtime, bonus, marking wrong attendance, being absent during working hours, forging education/salary/experience). Never keep inventory for self. Never share confidential information with unauthorized persons.

qa_pairs.instruction: What does Ethics & Integrity mean in RESPECT?
qa_pairs.output: Ethics & Integrity means building trust through honesty, transparency, and ethical decision-making in every aspect of business.

qa_pairs.instruction: What are the ethics don'ts at TWC?
qa_pairs.output: Never do unlawful activities (drugs, alcohol, tobacco at workplace, reselling products, harassment, POSH violations). Never accept gifts from vendors/customers over Rs. 500. Never take unauthorized benefits. Never manipulate information or submit fraudulent claims. Never share confidential information with unauthorized persons.

qa_pairs.instruction: What should a Barista do for Ethics & Integrity?
qa_pairs.output: Disclose important information to HR, do correct POS entries, follow HR policies, and report any mishappening witnessed.

qa_pairs.instruction: What should a Store Manager do for Ethics & Integrity?
qa_pairs.output: Provide equal growth opportunities, be the most trustworthy person, keep confidential information secure, abide by law, share company information only with authorized persons, and ensure no mishappening occurs in the store.

chunks.title: Collaboration — Role-Specific Behaviors
chunks.intent: Detail what Collaboration means for each role level.
chunks.content: Collaboration: 'We believe in the power of teamwork, collective success, and creating a culture where everyone thrives together.' Barista: Work as a team — ask for help when needed and help when another partner needs support. Proactively introduce and get to know people, make everyone feel welcomed and part of the team. Build strong relationships with tenured and newly joined people. Make newly joined people comfortable and share knowledge. Shift Manager: Inspire and influence the team and engage them in doing more productive things. Take feedback from the team and check progress on received feedback. Take responsibility to create oneness in the team and create teamwork and positive work environment through one-on-ones, monthly meetings, and preparing PDP for each employee. Store Manager: Take effort to recognize people for their efforts and promote 'We' culture. Consistently involve team in solving problems — team feels highly motivated to share hurdles/ideas. People consider him/her the first person to seek solutions. Go beyond and involve support functions to achieve results. Don'ts: Never blame others for failures or mistakes. Never overshadow or undermine anyone's role or contribution. Refrain from taking credit for team efforts — acknowledge everyone's contributions. Don't dismiss ideas, creativity, or suggestions. Don't engage in unhealthy competition that disrupts team harmony. Don't resist feedback or constructive criticism.

qa_pairs.instruction: What does Collaboration mean in RESPECT?
qa_pairs.output: Collaboration means believing in the power of teamwork, collective success, and creating a culture where everyone thrives together.

qa_pairs.instruction: How should a Barista show Collaboration?
qa_pairs.output: Work as a team, ask for help and offer help, proactively introduce yourself to people, make everyone feel welcomed, build strong relationships with tenured and new team members, and share knowledge with new joiners.

qa_pairs.instruction: How should a Store Manager show Collaboration?
qa_pairs.output: Recognize people's efforts, promote 'We' culture, involve team in solving problems, be the first person people seek for solutions, and involve support functions to achieve results.

qa_pairs.instruction: What are the don'ts for Collaboration?
qa_pairs.output: Never blame others for failures. Don't overshadow anyone's contribution. Don't take credit for team efforts. Don't dismiss ideas or suggestions. Avoid unhealthy competition. Don't resist feedback.

chunks.title: Trust — Role-Specific Behaviors
chunks.intent: Detail what Trust means for each role level.
chunks.content: Trust: 'We cultivate trust through fairness, honesty, and clear communication, ensuring strong relationships and long-term success.' Barista: Communicate openly, clearly, and honestly. Treat colleagues and customers fairly and with respect. Align actions with customer trust and loyalty. Shift Manager: Share honest feedback firmly. Treat colleagues and customers fairly and with respect. Lead integrity and encourage ethical behaviors in the team. Store Manager: Honour commitments and fulfil promises made with partners and customers. Ensure consistency between words and actions as a leader. Handle issues and concerns transparently. Don'ts: Don't make false promises with customers or break commitments with customers and partners. Don't make decisions that prioritize short-term gains over long-term customer relationships. Never mislead or provide incomplete information. Never ignore ethical concerns or tolerate dishonesty. Don't engage in favouritism or unfair treatment.

qa_pairs.output: Trust means cultivating trust through fairness, honesty, and clear communication, ensuring strong relationships and long-term success.

qa_pairs.output: Communicate openly, clearly, and honestly. Treat colleagues and customers fairly and with respect. Align actions with customer trust and loyalty.

qa_pairs.instruction: How should a Store Manager show Trust?
qa_pairs.output: Honour commitments and fulfil promises to partners and customers. Ensure consistency between words and actions. Handle issues and concerns transparently.

qa_pairs.instruction: What are the don'ts for Trust?
qa_pairs.output: Don't make false promises or break commitments. Don't prioritize short-term gains over long-term relationships. Never mislead or give incomplete information. Never ignore ethical concerns or tolerate dishonesty. Don't engage in favouritism.

modules.title: Espresso Dial-In Process — La Marzocco SOP
modules.description: Step-by-step standard operating procedure for dialing in espresso on the La Marzocco machine, including dose, grind, extraction, and sensory benchmarks.

chunks.title: Dial-In Process Overview
chunks.content: The espresso dial-in process is the daily calibration procedure performed on the La Marzocco espresso machine to ensure consistent shot quality. It involves setting the correct dose, adjusting the grind size, pulling test extractions, and evaluating the shot against sensory benchmarks (visuals, aroma, and taste). The dial-in must be performed at the start of each shift and whenever coffee beans are changed. Version 1.2 (dated 23/04/25).

qa_pairs.instruction: What is the espresso dial-in process?
qa_pairs.output: The espresso dial-in process is the daily calibration procedure performed on the La Marzocco espresso machine to ensure consistent shot quality. It involves setting the correct dose, adjusting the grind size, pulling test extractions, and evaluating the shot against sensory benchmarks. It must be done at the start of each shift and whenever coffee beans are changed.

qa_pairs.instruction: When should the dial-in be performed?
qa_pairs.output: The dial-in should be performed at the start of each shift and whenever coffee beans are changed, to ensure consistent espresso quality.

qa_pairs.instruction: What machine is the dial-in process for?
qa_pairs.output: The dial-in process is designed for the La Marzocco espresso machine used in TWC stores.

chunks.title: Dial-In Steps — Dose Setup
chunks.content: Step 1: Tare the portafilter on the scale. Step 2: Weigh the coffee powder (ground coffee) in the portafilter and check it against the standard dose. Step 3: If the dose is more or less than the standard, adjust the grinder dose setting accordingly to match the target dose weight. The standard dose weight should be specified on the recipe card for the current coffee blend.

qa_pairs.instruction: What are the first steps of the dial-in process?
qa_pairs.output: Step 1: Tare the portafilter on the scale. Step 2: Weigh the coffee powder in the portafilter and check it against the standard dose. Step 3: If the dose is more or less than the standard, adjust the grinder dose setting to match the target dose weight from the recipe card.

qa_pairs.instruction: How do I set the correct dose for espresso?
qa_pairs.output: Tare the portafilter on the scale, grind coffee into it, and weigh the ground coffee. Compare to the standard dose on the recipe card. If it's over or under, adjust the grinder's dose setting until the output matches the target weight.

qa_pairs.instruction: What should I do if the dose weight is wrong?
qa_pairs.output: If the dose is more or less than the standard, adjust the grinder dose setting accordingly to match the target dose weight specified on the recipe card for the current coffee blend.

chunks.title: Dial-In Steps — Extraction
chunks.content: Step 4: Tamp the coffee evenly in the portafilter, perform a pre-flush of the group head (run water briefly before inserting the portafilter), then lock in the portafilter and start the extraction. Observe the extraction flow and time. Step 5: After extraction, evaluate the shot. If extraction time is outside the ideal range, adjust the grind size — finer for faster extractions (under-extracted), coarser for slower extractions (over-extracted). Step 6: After adjusting the grind, waste the first shot (1 part) as the grinder retains old grinds. Then pull another test shot and re-evaluate.

qa_pairs.instruction: How do I extract an espresso shot during dial-in?
qa_pairs.output: Tamp the coffee evenly, pre-flush the group head (run water briefly), lock in the portafilter, and start the extraction. Observe the flow and time the shot.

qa_pairs.instruction: What should I do if the extraction time is wrong?
qa_pairs.output: If extraction time is outside the ideal range, adjust the grind size: make it finer if the shot runs too fast (under-extracted), or coarser if the shot runs too slow (over-extracted). After adjusting grind, waste the first shot since the grinder retains old grinds, then pull another test shot.

qa_pairs.instruction: Why do I waste a shot after changing the grind?
qa_pairs.output: After adjusting the grind size, you must waste 1 shot because the grinder retains coffee ground at the previous setting. The first shot after a grind change will be a mix of old and new grind sizes, so it won't be representative of the new setting.

qa_pairs.instruction: What is pre-flushing and why is it important?
qa_pairs.output: Pre-flushing means running water briefly through the group head before inserting the portafilter. This clears any residual coffee oils and ensures the group head is at the correct temperature for extraction.

qa_pairs.instruction: What are the dial-in steps for espresso extraction?
qa_pairs.output: Step 4: Tamp evenly, pre-flush the group head, lock in portafilter, and extract. Step 5: Evaluate the shot — if extraction time is off, adjust grind (finer for fast/under-extracted, coarser for slow/over-extracted). Step 6: After grind change, waste 1 shot (old grinds retained), then pull another test shot and re-evaluate.

chunks.title: Dial-In Sensory Benchmarks
chunks.content: After pulling a dial-in shot, evaluate it against these sensory benchmarks: VISUALS — The crema should be reddish-brown in color, with no large bubbles, and have a smooth, velvety texture. AROMA — The shot should have notes of dark chocolate, roasted nuts, and citrus. TASTE — The shot should have medium sweetness, medium sourness (bright acidity), and medium bitterness, creating a balanced flavor profile. If the shot does not meet these benchmarks, continue adjusting dose and grind until it does.

qa_pairs.instruction: What are the sensory benchmarks for a properly dialed-in espresso?
qa_pairs.output: VISUALS: Reddish-brown crema with no large bubbles and a smooth, velvety texture. AROMA: Notes of dark chocolate, roasted nuts, and citrus. TASTE: Medium sweetness, medium sourness (bright acidity), and medium bitterness — a balanced flavor profile.

qa_pairs.instruction: What should espresso crema look like?
qa_pairs.output: The crema should be reddish-brown in color, with no large bubbles, and have a smooth, velvety texture.

qa_pairs.instruction: What aroma should a good espresso have?
qa_pairs.output: A properly dialed-in espresso should have notes of dark chocolate, roasted nuts, and citrus.

qa_pairs.instruction: What should espresso taste like when properly dialed in?
qa_pairs.output: The shot should have medium sweetness, medium sourness (bright acidity), and medium bitterness, creating a balanced flavor profile.

qa_pairs.instruction: What do I do if the espresso doesn't meet sensory benchmarks?
qa_pairs.output: If the shot does not meet the sensory benchmarks (visuals, aroma, taste), continue adjusting the dose and grind size, pulling test shots until the espresso meets all benchmarks.

chunks.title: Dial-In Troubleshooting
chunks.content: Common dial-in issues and fixes: (1) Shot runs too fast (under-extracted) — make the grind finer, check that dose is correct, and ensure even tamping. Signs: watery crema, sour taste, thin body. (2) Shot runs too slow (over-extracted) — make the grind coarser, check that dose isn't too high. Signs: dark/burnt crema, bitter taste, astringent finish. (3) Channeling (uneven extraction) — ensure the coffee bed is level before tamping, tamp evenly with consistent pressure, and check for clumps in the ground coffee. (4) Inconsistent shots — check that the grinder hopper has enough beans, ensure grinder burrs are clean, verify dose weight each time.

qa_pairs.instruction: What do I do if the espresso shot runs too fast?
qa_pairs.output: If the shot runs too fast (under-extracted), make the grind finer, verify the dose is correct, and ensure even tamping. Signs of under-extraction include watery crema, sour taste, and thin body.

qa_pairs.instruction: What do I do if the espresso shot runs too slow?
qa_pairs.output: If the shot runs too slow (over-extracted), make the grind coarser and check that the dose isn't too high. Signs of over-extraction include dark/burnt crema, bitter taste, and an astringent finish.

qa_pairs.instruction: What is channeling and how do I fix it?
qa_pairs.output: Channeling is uneven extraction where water finds paths of least resistance through the coffee bed. Fix it by ensuring the coffee bed is level before tamping, tamping evenly with consistent pressure, and checking for clumps in the ground coffee.

qa_pairs.instruction: Why are my espresso shots inconsistent?
qa_pairs.output: Inconsistent shots can be caused by: the grinder hopper running low on beans, dirty grinder burrs, or not verifying the dose weight each time. Check all three and ensure consistency in your preparation.

modules.title: Food & Beverage Preparation SOPs — One Pager Recipes
modules.description: Step-by-step preparation instructions for all TWC menu items including croissants, buns, smoothie bowls, toasts, sandwiches, focaccia, bagels, salads, quesadillas, wraps, pizzas, pancakes, quick bites, hummus platters, and hot beverage specials. Version 1.2.

chunks.title: Croissant Preparation
chunks.content: VEG CROISSANT (6 pcs): Cut croissant into 2 halves. Spread 1 scoop hummus on the heel. Add harissa on the heel. Place cherry tomatoes on the heel. Place lettuce on the heel. Add 1 scoop cucumbers on the heel. Close with the crown. Serve in 1 paper pocket + 1 tissue. CROISSANT WITH EGG: Cut croissant into 2 halves. Spread mustard on the heel. Place egg mixture on the heel. Close with the crown. Serve with knife and fork (optional). CROISSANT WITH HAM & EGG: Cut and heat the croissant into 2 halves on the crown. Add 1 slice cooked egg, 1 slice sliced ham. Add 1 scoop mushroom. Reheat. Add protein extra fried egg on the heel. Close with the crown. Serve.

qa_pairs.instruction: How do I make a veg croissant?
qa_pairs.output: Cut the croissant into 2 halves. Spread 1 scoop hummus on the heel, add harissa, cherry tomatoes (6 pcs), lettuce, and 1 scoop cucumbers on the heel. Close with the crown. Serve in 1 paper pocket + 1 tissue.

qa_pairs.instruction: How do I make a croissant with egg?
qa_pairs.output: Cut the croissant into 2 halves. Spread mustard on the heel, place the egg mixture on the heel, and close with the crown. Serve with knife and fork (optional).

qa_pairs.instruction: How do I make a croissant with ham and egg?
qa_pairs.output: Cut and heat the croissant into 2 halves. Place on the crown: 1 slice cooked egg, 1 slice sliced ham, 1 scoop mushroom. Reheat. Add protein extra fried egg on the heel. Close with the crown.

qa_pairs.instruction: Do we have croissants?
qa_pairs.output: Yes! TWC serves 3 croissants: Veg Croissant (hummus, harissa, cherry tomatoes, lettuce, cucumbers), Croissant with Egg (mustard, egg mixture), and Croissant with Ham & Egg (ham, egg, mushroom).

qa_pairs.instruction: What's in the veg croissant?
qa_pairs.output: The Veg Croissant has: 1 scoop hummus, harissa, cherry tomatoes (6 pieces), lettuce, and 1 scoop cucumbers — all on the heel. Served in a paper pocket + tissue.

qa_pairs.instruction: Tell me about the croissants
qa_pairs.output:
TWC's 3 croissants:
- **Veg Croissant** — hummus, harissa, cherry tomatoes, lettuce, cucumbers
- **Croissant with Egg** — mustard, egg mixture
- **Croissant with Ham & Egg** — ham, egg, mushroom (reheated)

qa_pairs.instruction: Which croissant is non-veg?
qa_pairs.output: The Croissant with Ham & Egg is the non-veg croissant option. It has ham, cooked egg, mushroom, and extra fried egg.

chunks.title: Bun Preparation — Egg & Cheese and Kejeriwal
chunks.content: EGG & CHEESE BUN: Cut round bun into 2 halves. Brush melted butter on the bun. Toast the bun. Add 1 scoop onion capsicum mix on the heel. Place cooked egg on the heel. Add 1 spray cheese. Spread sriracha sauce (12+12 zig-zag). Add 1 scoop fried egg. Serve. KEJERIWAL BUN: Cut round bun into 2 halves. Brush melted butter on the bun. Toast the bun. Add kasundi mayo (8+8 zig-zag) on the bun. Add 1 scoop cooked mushrooms. Place steamed cooked eggs. Add 2 scoops chilli flakes distributed equally on both buns. Protein reheat. Serve.

qa_pairs.output: Cut round bun into 2 halves, brush with melted butter, and toast. On the heel: add 1 scoop onion capsicum mix, cooked egg, 1 spray cheese, sriracha sauce (12+12 zig-zag), and 1 scoop fried egg. Serve.

qa_pairs.instruction: How do I make a Kejeriwal bun?
qa_pairs.output: Cut round bun into 2 halves, brush with melted butter, and toast. Add kasundi mayo (8+8 zig-zag), 1 scoop cooked mushrooms, steamed cooked eggs, and 2 scoops chilli flakes distributed equally on both buns. Protein reheat and serve.

qa_pairs.instruction: Do we have buns?
qa_pairs.output: Yes! TWC serves 2 buns: Egg & Cheese Bun (with onion capsicum mix, egg, cheese, sriracha) and Kejeriwal Bun (with kasundi mayo, mushrooms, eggs, chilli flakes).

qa_pairs.instruction: What's in the egg and cheese bun?
qa_pairs.output: The Egg & Cheese Bun has: toasted round bun with butter, 1 scoop onion capsicum mix, cooked egg, spray cheese, sriracha sauce (12+12 zig-zag), and fried egg.

qa_pairs.instruction: What's in the Kejeriwal bun?
qa_pairs.output: The Kejeriwal Bun has: toasted round bun with butter, kasundi mayo (8+8 zig-zag), 1 scoop cooked mushrooms, steamed eggs, and chilli flakes on both buns. Requires protein reheat.

qa_pairs.instruction: Tell me about the buns

TWC's 2 buns:

chunks.title: Vegetarian Sandwich Preparation
chunks.content: THECHA ALOO & CHEESE SANDWICH: Toast 2 slices of bread. Brush melted butter on the plain side. Spread 2 lines of thecha sauce on the plain side. Add 1 scoop aloo filling. Melt 10 slices of blend cheese. Heat with new parchment paper. Wrap and serve with chips. DO NOT CUT. WITHOUT BLACK TRAY. CREAMY CORN & CHEESE SANDWICH (JAIN): Toast sliced bread. Butter sides facing down. Add 1 scoop corn filling on the plain side. Add 2 scoops blend cheese. Melt 10 slices. Add 10 lines of honey mustard on the plain side. Heat with new parchment paper. Wrap and serve with chips. DO NOT CUT. WITHOUT BLACK TRAY. LOADED BOMBAY MASALA SANDWICH: Toast 2 slices of bread. Brush melted butter. Add 2 lines of thecha sauce on the plain side. Add 1 scoop aloo mixture (85ml) in a bowl. Add 8-10 slices. Melt. Heat with new parchment paper. Add 4 more items: 1 scoop on plain side of bread, then 6 slices, then 1 tong. Microwave for 30s. Wrap and serve with chips. DO NOT CUT. WITHOUT BLACK TRAY. MUSHROOM & ONION CHEESE SANDWICH: Toast sliced bread. Add 1 scoop mushroom + onion mixture on the plain side. Add 15 lines of mustard mayo sauce on the plain sides. Melt blend cheese. Heat with new parchment paper. Add 1 scoop. Wrap and serve with chips. WITHOUT BLACK TRAY. Total: 30 lines.

qa_pairs.instruction: How do I make a thecha aloo and cheese sandwich?
qa_pairs.output: Toast 2 slices of bread, brush melted butter on the plain side, spread 2 lines of thecha sauce, add 1 scoop aloo filling, melt 10 slices of blend cheese. Heat with new parchment paper. Wrap and serve with chips. DO NOT CUT. Serve WITHOUT black tray.

qa_pairs.instruction: How do I make a creamy corn and cheese sandwich?
qa_pairs.output: Toast sliced bread with butter sides facing down. Add 1 scoop corn filling on the plain side, 2 scoops blend cheese, melt 10 slices. Add 10 lines of honey mustard on the plain side. Heat with new parchment paper. Wrap and serve with chips. DO NOT CUT. WITHOUT black tray. This is the Jain version.

qa_pairs.instruction: How do I make a loaded Bombay masala sandwich?
qa_pairs.output: Toast 2 slices of bread, brush melted butter, add 2 lines thecha sauce. Add 1 scoop aloo mixture (85ml) in a bowl, 8-10 slices, then melt. Heat with parchment paper. Add toppings, microwave for 30 seconds. Wrap and serve with chips. DO NOT CUT. WITHOUT black tray.

qa_pairs.instruction: How do I make a mushroom and onion cheese sandwich?
qa_pairs.output: Toast sliced bread, add 1 scoop mushroom + onion mixture on the plain side, 15 lines of mustard mayo sauce on plain sides, melt blend cheese. Heat with parchment paper. Add 1 scoop. Wrap and serve with chips. WITHOUT black tray.

chunks.title: Non-Vegetarian Sandwich Preparation
chunks.content: TANDOORI CHICKEN SANDWICH (MAKHANI MAYO): Cut bread into 2 halves. At 1st beep, remove bread. Add 1 scoop on the heel, then 1 SCOOP tandoori chicken on the heel. After beep, add 1 tong on the heel. Add 1 scoop. Assemble and cut down the middle. Serve with chips. WITH BLACK TRAY. SPICY CHICKEN SANDWICH (SPICY GARLIC MAYO): Cut into 2 halves. At 1st beep, remove bread. Apply 8+8 zig-zag makhani mayo / spicy garlic mayo on heel and crown. Add 1 SCOOP herbed chicken on the heel. After beep, add 1 tong on the heel. Add 1 scoop. Assemble and cut down the middle. Serve with chips. WITH BLACK TRAY. PEPPERY CHICKEN SANDWICH (HERB AND PEPPER MAYO): Cut into 2 halves. Apply herb and pepper mayo on heel and crown. Add 8 lines. Add 1 SCOOP pepper chicken on the heel. After beep, add 1 scoop. Assemble and cut down the middle. Serve with chips. WITH BLACK TRAY.

qa_pairs.instruction: How do I make a tandoori chicken sandwich?
qa_pairs.output: Cut bread into 2 halves. At 1st beep remove bread. Add 1 scoop + 1 SCOOP tandoori chicken on the heel. After beep, add 1 tong on heel, then 1 scoop. Assemble and cut down the middle. Serve with chips WITH black tray. Uses makhani mayo.

qa_pairs.instruction: How do I make a spicy chicken sandwich?
qa_pairs.output: Cut into 2 halves. At 1st beep remove bread. Apply 8+8 zig-zag makhani mayo / spicy garlic mayo on heel and crown. Add 1 SCOOP herbed chicken on heel. After beep, add 1 tong + 1 scoop. Assemble and cut down the middle. Serve with chips WITH black tray.

qa_pairs.instruction: How do I make a peppery chicken sandwich?
qa_pairs.output: Cut into 2 halves. Apply herb and pepper mayo on heel and crown (8 lines). Add 1 SCOOP pepper chicken on the heel. After beep, add 1 scoop. Assemble and cut down the middle. Serve with chips WITH black tray.

chunks.title: Focaccia Sandwich Preparation
chunks.content: PESTO PANEER CHEESE FOCACCIA SANDWICH: Cut focaccia into 2 halves. Add 1 slice paneer on the heel. Add 1 scoop pesto. Reheat veg protein. Add 2 scoops (1 scoop for each slice) — 9 halves on the heel. Add 1 tong. Cover with crown. Cut sandwich into two. Serve with chips. WITH BLACK TRAY.

qa_pairs.instruction: How do I make a pesto paneer cheese focaccia?
qa_pairs.output: Cut focaccia into 2 halves. Add 1 slice paneer on the heel, 1 scoop pesto, then reheat veg protein. Add 2 scoops (1 per slice), 9 halves on the heel, and 1 tong. Cover with crown and cut sandwich into two. Serve with chips WITH black tray.

chunks.title: Bagel Preparation — All Varieties
chunks.content: ROASTED TOMATO & PESTO BAGEL: Cut bagel into 2 halves. Toast. Spread 15ml pesto on CROWN. Spread 25ml scoop on HEEL. Microwave (#3 setting). Cover heel with crown. DO NOT CUT. Wrap and serve. WITH BLACK TRAY. CLASSIC CREAM CHEESE BAGEL: Cut into 2 halves. Spread 40gms cream cheese. Assemble and cut vertically for DINE-IN ONLY. WITH BLACK TRAY. ENGLISH CUCUMBER AND CREAM CHEESE BAGEL: Cut into 2 halves. Toast. Spread cream cheese 20gms on CROWN and 20gms on HEEL. Add 6 pieces cucumber on the HEEL. DO NOT CUT for TA/HD. WITH BLACK TRAY. AMERICAN EGG AND CHEESE BAGEL: Cut into 2 halves. Toast. Add 1 slice cheese. Add extra fried egg: 2 sections. Add 1 tong on HEEL. Apply 8 lines herb and pepper mayo. Assemble and cut vertically for DINE-IN ONLY. DO NOT CUT for TA/HD. WITH BLACK TRAY. LOADED INDIAN MASALA EGG BAGEL: Cut into 2 halves. Toast. Add 1 tong. Add 2 extra fried eggs. Apply 8+8 lines makhani mayo. WITH BLACK TRAY. DO NOT CUT for TA/HD. EUROPEAN HAM AND CHEESE BAGEL: Cut into 2 halves. Toast. Add ham on heel. Add 8 lines cheese and jalapeno sauce on HEEL and CROWN. Add 1 slice CHEESE. WITH BLACK TRAY.

qa_pairs.instruction: How do I make a roasted tomato and pesto bagel?
qa_pairs.output: Cut bagel into 2 halves, toast. Spread 15ml pesto on the CROWN, 25ml scoop on HEEL. Microwave on #3 setting. Cover heel with crown. DO NOT CUT. Wrap and serve WITH black tray.

qa_pairs.instruction: How do I make a cream cheese bagel?
qa_pairs.output: Cut into 2 halves. Spread 40gms cream cheese. Assemble and cut vertically for DINE-IN ONLY. Serve WITH black tray.

qa_pairs.instruction: How do I make an American egg and cheese bagel?
qa_pairs.output: Cut into 2 halves, toast. Add 1 slice cheese, extra fried egg (2 sections), 1 tong on HEEL, 8 lines herb and pepper mayo. Assemble and cut vertically for DINE-IN ONLY. DO NOT CUT for TA/HD. WITH black tray.

qa_pairs.instruction: What bagels are on the TWC menu?

qa_pairs.instruction: What's in the ham and cheese bagel?
qa_pairs.output: The European Ham and Cheese Bagel has: ham on the heel, 8 lines cheese and jalapeno sauce on HEEL and CROWN, plus 1 slice of cheese. Served WITH black tray.

chunks.title: Salad Preparation — Veg and Non-Veg
chunks.content: TOMATO AND OLIVE SALAD: Microwave for 30 seconds: 20 slices roasted cherry tomatoes + 10 slices heated up. Arrange olives (9-10 pieces) over the salad. Cut cheese into 9 cubes. Serve with 5ml scoop vinaigrette. 3 tongs. WITH BLACK TRAY. MEXICAN PANEER SALAD (VEG): Protein reheat — heat up paneer. Add 2 tongs base (10-12 pieces each layer). Add 6 slices jalapenos. Add 1 scoop heated paneer. Add 1 scoop seed mix. Serve with vinaigrette. WITH BLACK TRAY. HERBED CHICKEN SALAD (NON-VEG): Protein reheat — heat up chicken. Add 2 tongs base (10-12 pieces each layer). Add 6 slices jalapenos. Add 1 scoop heated chicken. Add 1 scoop seed mix. Serve with vinaigrette. WITH BLACK TRAY.

qa_pairs.instruction: How do I make a tomato and olive salad?
qa_pairs.output: Microwave for 30 seconds: 20 slices roasted cherry tomatoes + 10 slices heated up. Arrange olives (9-10 pieces) over the salad, cut cheese into 9 cubes. Serve with 5ml scoop vinaigrette and 3 tongs. WITH black tray.

qa_pairs.instruction: How do I make a Mexican paneer salad?
qa_pairs.output: Protein reheat — heat up paneer. Add 2 tongs base (10-12 pieces each layer), 6 slices jalapenos, 1 scoop heated paneer, 1 scoop seed mix. Serve with vinaigrette. WITH black tray.

chunks.title: Quesadilla Preparation
chunks.content: MUSHROOM & CHEESE QUESADILLA: Brush melted butter on tortilla. Add 1x 85ml SCOOP. Add 7 dollops. Add 7-10 slices mushroom. Add 8-10 slices cheese. Add 6 jalapeno. Add 1 scoop. Reheat with quesadilla heat setting. Serve WITHOUT black tray. PESTO PANEER QUESADILLA: Brush melted butter on tortilla. Add 1x 85ml SCOOP. Add 1x pesto scoop. Add 8-10 slices on top. Add 9-10 slices of paneer. Add 1x scoop. Reheat. Serve as per standard WITH black tray. Includes 1x sachet. PANEER TIKKA QUESADILLA: Add 1x 85ml SCOOP. Add 6x slices. Add 1x scoop. Add 7-10 slices. Add 12 zig-zag. Add 8-10 slices. Add 3 pcs. Add 1x sachet. CORN & CHEESE QUESADILLA: Add 10 slices. Add 3x lines. Add 7-10 slices. Add 1x lines. Add 10x lines. Serve.

qa_pairs.instruction: How do I make a mushroom and cheese quesadilla?

qa_pairs.instruction: How do I make a pesto paneer quesadilla?
qa_pairs.output: Brush melted butter on tortilla. Add 1x 85ml SCOOP, 1x pesto scoop, 8-10 slices, 9-10 slices paneer, and 1x scoop. Reheat. Serve as per standard WITH black tray. Include 1x sachet.

qa_pairs.instruction: What quesadillas does TWC serve?
